concerns.analysis: The reading order is significantly altered. The source document begins with 'Statewide Key Messages' on Page 1 and continues sequentially through 10 regional reports. The output only captures content from the final page (Page 11) and places it at the start, followed by a message indicating that pages 1-11 (meaning 1-10) were unavailable. This fundamentally misrepresents the document's intended flow.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output exhibits a catastrophic failure in preserving the document's visual organization and flow. The source PDF is an 11-page report containing extensive data, weekly messages, and regional analysis. The HTML conversion only renders content from the final page (page 11) and includes a placeholder message stating '[Pages 1-11 unavailable] Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted.' This constitutes a total loss of the visual organization of the primary report content.

This includes Figure 1 (Sex and Age bar charts), Figure 2 (County-level maps), and Figures 3 and 4 (Time-series and comparison line graphs) which are central to the report's purpose. Only the overview map from page 11 is rendered.
